english: any thing in common with the Christian Trinity, nor even with the Indian Trimūrtti, for the first state, the 'body of law', the absolute body, is Buddha in the Nirvāṇa, the so-called first world of abstract existence i. e. non-existence, which is the ultimate aim and end of every existence and the ideal aspired to by every believing Buddhist; the second state, the 'body of happiness or glory' is Buddha in the perfection of a conscious and active life of bliss in the second world (heaven or Elysium), which state however is inferior to the first; the third, the 'body of transformation and incarnation', is Buddha in the third or visible world, as man on earth. Notwithstanding the altogether abstract character of čós-sku (chos sku), as a philosophical conception, Buddhistic fancy is pleased to represent it as a visible image of Buddha, shining in the colours of the rainbow, or at least as a brilliant apparition of light, though impalpable and unapproachable; and this is not only a notion of the vulgar, but is acknowledged also in literature. More recent speculators have even added a ṅo-bo-ñyíd-kyi sku (ngo bo nyid kyi sku) superior to the three, viz. that which is eternal in the essence of a Buddha, even čós-sku (chos sku), the absolute body, being described by these philosophers as transient. The unintelligible passage in Cs.'s dictionary, p. 305 b. might be corrected thus: adding to the former three as a fourth' etc. - To this signification belong the compounds sku-rím (sku rim), resp. for rim-gro (rim gro) reverence, respect, particularly in the special sense of a solemn sacrificial ceremony, performed on public and private occasions, e. g. in cases of disease; sku-rím byéd-pa (sku rim byed pa) to perform such a ceremony. - sku-rtén (sku rten), sku-tsáb (sku tshab), sku-gzugs (sku gzugs), sku-'drá (sku 'dra) (W. *kun-ḍá*) image of Buddha etc. - 3. image, statue, of Buddha or other holy persons, gsér-sku (gser sku) a gold image, rdó-sku (rdo sku) a stone image, 'jim-sku ('jim sku) an image of clay, bris-sku (bris sku) a painted image, 'búr-sku ('bur sku) a basso-relievo, rkós-sku (rkos sku) an engraved, blúgs- (blugs) or ldugs-sku (ldugs sku) a molten, tágs-sku (thags sku) a woven image Cs. - sku-'búm (sku 'bum) 'mausoleum' or acc. to another etymology 'the 100 000 images', n. of the famous monastery Kumbúm east of the Kokonor (v. Travels of Huc and Gabet). - sku gsúṅ túgs (sku gsung thugs) 1. (cf. sku (sku) no. 1) resp. f. lus (lus) ṅag (ngag) yíd (yid) the three spheres of a man's doings or sufferings, works, words and thoughts. - 2. the rtén gsum (rten gsum), the three representations of Buddha: the image of his person, the books containing his doctrine, the pyramid (mčód-rtén) (mchod rten) as the symbol of his grace. - sku-lṅa-rgyál-po (sku lnga rgyal po) five deities of degenerated Buddhism Schl. 157.

a paddle-wheel, without a rim; such are the water-wheels of all the mills in the Himalaya skú-ru-k'a (sku ru kha) the figure of a cross + X. The latter is common in books as an abbreviation like our 'etc.', to save the repeated writing at full length of the same sentence, as refrains etc.
the stake in a game or wager received by the winner. - skugs-stóṅ (skugs stong) Sch. id.?

pf. bskuṅs (bskungs), ft. bskuṅ (bskung) 1. to hide in the ground. - 2. to bury, to inter. - 3. to tie in a doubled or twisted position, e. g. a corpse before it is burnt, to cord on all sides. - bskúṅs-sa (bskungs sa) lurking-place, hiding-place Mil.

I. sbst. thread, yarn; wire; skúd-pa gcód-pa (skud pa gcod pa) to cut off the thread, also fig. Cs. to divorce; ras-skúd (ras skud) cotton thread, lcágs-skúd (lcags skud) iron wire; tson-skúd (tshon skud) coloured thread; skud-ró (skud ro) the thread-ends of a seam; skúd-bris-mk'an (skud bris mkhan) an embroiderer. II. vb. pf. bskus (bskus), ft. bsku (bsku), imp. skus (skus), col. kú-wa (ku wa) C. *skú-če* (*sku che*) W. to smear *tá-gir-la mar skú-če* (*ta gir la mar sku che*) to butter the bread W., *di-la nág-po ma sku* (*di la nag po ma sku*) don't make that dirty W.; to besmear, to daub sñyíṅ-po(-la) snúm-gyis (snying po (la) snum gyis) a wick with grease Dzl.; sgó-la rtsi (sgo la rtsi) to paint a door; spós-kyis skúd-pa (spos kyis skud pa) to anoint; skud (skud) ointment, *śra-skúd* (*shra skud*) pomatum W.
